item.title: Detroit Is the Last City That Knows How to Dance
item.description: Dance floors are flooded with selfies and poseurs. But in one city, it’s still a place to feel free.

item.title: Sonny Rollins Deserves a Bridge Named After Him
item.description: Sonny Rollins was a giant, New York born and bred. And he has a particularly meaningful relationship to the Williamsburg Bridge.

item.title: MAGA’s French Connection
item.description: This is MAGA’s French connection.

item.title: What the Cult of Efficiency Costs Us
item.description: The Save Our Bacon Act is bad for pigs and bad for us.

item.title: The New Isolation of the Israeli Cultural Scene
item.description: In Tel Aviv, there’s a feeling that “we are on our own.”

item.title: About the Meat Industry: Before We Take a Bite, Hard Questions
item.description: Readers respond to a guest essay and a column by Nicholas Kristof about the meat industry.

item.title: The Tiny Solar Panel That Could Change America
item.description: I hope balcony solar’s charisma and low cost help us imagine the energy-abundant future we are so close to achieving.

item.title: Bob Dylan and Liza Minnelli Already Turned 80. They Have Thoughts for Trump.
item.description: “I feel the same as I did 50 years ago,” President Trump said. “It’s crazy.”

item.title: The U.S. Is the Future of Soccer, for Better or Worse
item.description: Thanks to immigrants and the internet, Americans gained entry to a universe beyond our borders.

item.title: Only One President Could Come Up With This Spectacle
item.description: Trump has gone beyond kayfabe.

item.title: Jon Ossoff’s Anti-Authoritarian Playbook
item.description: The Opinion columnist Michelle Goldberg returned from a recent trip to Hungary with an appreciation for Senator Jon Ossoff’s playbook. Viktor Orban’s stunning defeat demonstrates the power of an anticorruption message, she argues, and Ossoff offers a model for Democrats.

item.title: I’m a 53-Year-Old Woman. Stop Telling Me I’m Invisible.
item.description: There’s never been a better time to be a middle-aged woman.

item.title: Does U.F.O. Disclosure Threaten Faith?
item.description: Steven Spielberg’s ‘Disclosure Day’ and the God-and-aliens debate.

item.title: How to Kick SpaceX Out of Your 401(k)
item.description: It is impossible to beat the stock market. But if you want to divest your retirement of Elon Musk-related companies, here is the best way to do so.

item.title: The Spectacle of Trump at 80
item.description: An octogenarian president for a 250-year-old republic.

item.title: Turning to ChatGPT for Help Instead of Your Doctor
item.description: Readers react to a guest essay by a doctor who found ChatGPT useful in dealing with her own medical issues.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein Haunts the White House
item.description: A sleazy situation that Trump can’t wish away.

item.title: The Spectacle of Trump at 80
item.description: An octogenarian president for a 250-year-old republic.

item.title: Republicans Thought They Owned ‘Values.’ Not Anymore.
item.description: A new politics of morality is rising in the Democratic Party.

item.title: The Supreme Court Doesn’t Own the Constitution
item.description: It starts with “We the people” for a reason.

item.title: ‘Top Republicans Should Actually Be More Concerned’
item.description: This conservative insider isn’t so sure that the G.O.P. can “buck historical trends” in the midterms.

item.title: Your SpaceX Odds Are Not in Your Favor
item.description: To buy a share of SpaceX or not to buy? That is the trillion-dollar question. With its high share price, only one person stands to profit from the company going public. According to the Opinion senior staff editor Bill Saporito, you might as well purchase lottery tickets instead.

item.title: California’s Excuses Are Damaging Faith in Government
item.description: Taking a week to count votes is doing it wrong.

item.title: SpaceX’s I.P.O. Will Help Fulfill Elon Musk’s Political Vision of America
item.description: The SpaceX I.P.O. will enable its founder to build — and scale — his peculiar vision of society.

item.title: Trump’s Arch Would Mar Hallowed Ground
item.description: Readers respond to President Trump’s efforts to remake the capital. Also: The Knicks; a plan for Democrats; U.S. political flaws; voting in person.

item.title: This Could Be the Winning Issue for Democrats
item.description: A.I. is not inevitable.

item.title: ‘A Mighty Form of Communion’: Three Writers on the World Cup’s Strange Magic
item.description: There’s ample cause for celebration and consternation alike.

item.title: ‘All Men Are Created Equal’? Not Everyone Agrees.
item.description: Our founding creed has always had its critics.

item.title: Social Security Is Going Broke. Where Is the Outrage?
item.description: We’ve been here before, but never so close.

item.title: Some Leftists Are Boycotting This Film. Everyone Should Watch It.
item.description: The inanity of the leftists who’d censor such a film shouldn’t distract us from the right-wing nightmare it reveals.

item.title: I Was a V.C. Partner. We Can’t Let Silicon Valley Buy Democracy.
item.description: Pro-A.I. groups are spending gobs of cash to influence political campaigns. That’s terrible news for us all.

item.title: 5 Ingredients for a Healthy Life
item.description: Living a good life doesn’t have to be complicated, Christian Angermayer, founder of the Enhanced Games, tells the columnist Ross Douthat on “Interesting Times,” and shares five basics he believes are crucial for optimizing your time on earth.

item.title: ‘I Met God’
item.description: Psychedelics allow you to communicate with the divine, Christian Angermayer, founder of the Enhanced Games, tells the columnist Ross Douthat on “Interesting Times,” arguing that the safe use of certain mind-altering drugs should be part of living a full life.

item.title: Men Have Viagra. What About Women?
item.description: Modern medicine helped men have better sex, but what about women? Christian Angermayer, founder of the Enhanced Games, argues we’re entering a new era of “humanmaxxing,” in which scientific breakthroughs will help us have better sex, better sleep and level up our looks.

item.title: Better Sex, Better Hair, Better Sleep: ‘Humanmaxxing’ Is Here
item.description: The biotech billionaire who wants to rebuild your body and blow your mind.

item.title: Better Sex, Better Hair, Better Sleep: ‘Humanmaxxing’ Is Here
item.description: The biotech billionaire who wants to rebuild your body and blow your mind.

item.title: The Global Bull Market That A.I. Obscures
item.description: The rest of the world has been writing one of the more remarkable chapters in modern financial history — and many have hardly noticed.

item.title: Tom Steyer Spent $558 Million. What a Waste.
item.description: Imagine what Tom Steyer could have done with all the money he wasted on another campaign.

item.title: Spencer Pratt’s Defeat Isn’t Just About Los Angeles
item.description: Some of America’s largest cities are now being governed by progressives. What will we learn?

item.title: Should the Public Get a Piece of A.I. Wealth?
item.description: Readers respond to an Opinion guest essay by Senator Bernie Sanders. Also: President Trump’s enablers.

item.title: The Impossible Path Forward for Women in Politics
item.description: Republicans have spent years disparaging all things associated with women. Now Democrats are following suit.

item.title: The Terrible Man Theory of History
item.description: The solution to Trumpism isn’t to conform to the spirit of the age, but to transform it.

item.title: She Was One of Obama’s Top Lawyers. How Did She Get Tangled Up With Epstein?
item.description: That she, of all people, looked past his 2008 conviction tells you everything you need to know about how unreliable and corroded the legal world has become.

item.title: It’s Godzilla vs. T. Rex Out There All Over America
item.description: Is there nothing voters won’t forgive?

item.title: Cultivating a New Generation of Readers and a Love of Books
item.description: Readers respond to “Make America Read Again,” an Opinion guest essay by Brian Bannon. Also: Choosing Graham Platner; fading bank tellers.

item.title: With Bill Pulte, Trump Is Sending a Dangerous Message
item.description: President Trump’s decision to place Bill Pulte at the head of the U.S. intelligence community is not just another poor personnel choice. It’s a warning.

item.title: How Trump Weaponized the Antiwar Platform
item.description: Matt Duss, the executive vice president at the Center for International Policy, joins “The Ezra Klein Show” to discuss how President Trump successfully weaponized an antiwar platform that Democrats abandoned.

item.title: The A.I. Bubble Is Coming for Your Retirement Account
item.description: The public is about to buy into A.I.’s trillion-dollar promises. Are they worth the price?

item.title: Is the U.S.-Israel Alliance Still Strategic?
item.description: Is the U.S.-Israel alliance doing more harm than good? Matt Duss, the executive vice president at the Center for International Policy, joins “The Ezra Klein Show” to discuss whether the strategic and moral costs of the U.S.-Israel alliance outweigh the benefits.

item.title: For America’s 250th, Let’s Get Something Clear About Our National Identity
item.description: It depends on both ideas and cultural inheritance.
